macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Nasdaq Climbs Over 100 Points Despite Extreme Fear Index
items.summary: The Nasdaq Composite rose over 100 points on Tuesday amid high investor anxiety, with the Fear and Greed Index at 21.5 in 'Extreme Fear' zone. Markets await Federal Reserve's two-day policy meeting outcome, likely keeping rates near 3.75%, and upcoming PPI data.

items.title: S&P 500 Sectors Mixed; Energy and Consumer Discretionary Lead Gains
items.summary: Most S&P 500 sectors ended positively on Tuesday, driven by gains in energy, consumer discretionary, and communication services, while consumer staples and healthcare declined. Focus remains on Fed Chair Powell's statement on inflation and oil prices.

items.title: Brent Crude Falls Over 2% on Iraq-Turkey Oil Export Reopening
items.summary: Brent crude prices dropped more than 2% after Iraq reopened oil exports through Turkey's Ceyhan port, easing supply concerns from Iran conflict. This occurred despite ongoing market focus on Fed decisions.

items.title: Gold Steady Near $5,000 as Investors Eye Fed Rate-Cut Path
items.summary: Gold prices held steady around $5,000 per ounce as investors assess the Federal Reserve's potential rate-cut trajectory ahead of the policy meeting conclusion.

items.title: U.S. Stock Futures Rise Ahead of Fed Interest Rate Decision
items.summary: Futures on Nasdaq 100, S&P 500, and Dow Jones were up 0.62%, 0.48%, and 0.52% respectively early Wednesday as markets anticipate the Fed's rate decision and Powell's comments.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Trades Near $73,500–$73,900 Amid FOMC Anticipation and Institutional Inflows
items.summary: Bitcoin is holding strong at $73,500–$73,900, approaching $75,000 resistance ahead of the FOMC rate decision, supported by $1.1 billion in recent U.S. spot ETF inflows and MicroStrategy's ongoing accumulation strategy.

items.title: Aster Chain Launches Privacy-Focused Mainnet with Zero-Knowledge Encryption
items.summary: Aster, backed by YZi Labs, has launched its Layer-1 blockchain mainnet featuring stealth addresses to prevent front-running, with the ASTER token rallying over 5% to $0.76 and cross-chain support from Ethereum and Solana.

items.title: SEC and CFTC Clarify Most Crypto Assets Are Not Securities, Define Staking and Airdrops
items.summary: U.S. regulators SEC and CFTC have issued clarifications stating most crypto assets are not securities and provided definitions for staking, airdrops, and contracts, potentially easing regulatory uncertainty.

items.title: MicroStrategy Adds 22,337 BTC, Total Holdings Reach 761,068 BTC
items.summary: MicroStrategy raised $1.18 billion via preferred stock and acquired 22,337 more Bitcoin, bringing its total holdings to 761,068 BTC amid continued corporate accumulation trends.

items.title: GSR Acquires Autonomous and Architech for $57 Million
items.summary: Crypto trading firm GSR has acquired Autonomous and Architech for $57 million to bolster its infrastructure capabilities in the blockchain space.

items.title: Moody's Launches On-Chain Credit Ratings
items.summary: Moody's has introduced on-chain credit rating services, marking a significant step in integrating traditional credit assessments with blockchain technology.

items.title: PayPal Expands Stablecoins to 68 Additional Countries
items.summary: PayPal is rolling out its stablecoin services to 68 more countries, enhancing global access to crypto payments and stable value transfers.

items.title: Ethereum Trades Near $2,315 with Neutral Funding Rates
items.summary: Ethereum is stable at around $2,315 as funding rates normalize post-leverage flush, though a drop below $2,206 risks $1.156 billion in liquidations.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Multiverse Computing and Axelera AI Announce Strategic Collaboration for Edge AI Models
items.summary: Multiverse Computing and Axelera AI have launched a technology collaboration to integrate compressed AI models into Axelera’s Metis and Europa edge platforms, enabling efficient AI inference on low-power devices for applications in industrial, retail, mobility, and more.

items.title: NCS Partners with NVIDIA to Deploy Sovereign Agentic AI for Enterprises
items.summary: NCS, a Singtel subsidiary, has partnered with NVIDIA to deploy sovereign agentic AI solutions that allow enterprises to run AI agents on-premises or in the cloud while maintaining data security and governance for uses like service desks, video systems, and logistics.

items.title: TCS and Pearson Partner to Accelerate AI-Powered Learning for Enterprises
items.summary: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) and Pearson have announced a multi-year partnership to co-develop AI-enabled learning and assessment products, helping enterprises upskill workforces for AI-driven changes using contextual AI and digital platforms.

items.title: Embedded AI Market Projected to Reach USD 42.3 Billion by 2033
items.summary: The embedded AI market, valued at USD 13.8 billion in 2026, is expected to grow to USD 42.3 billion by 2033 at a 17.3% CAGR, driven by advancements in AI chipsets, NPUs, and edge deployment for real-time processing in IoT, robotics, and smart devices.

items.title: AI in Sports Market Forecast: Machine Learning to Dominate by 2030
items.summary: The AI in sports market is expanding with machine learning projected to hold 35% share by 2030, supported by performance analytics, injury prevention, and integrations with IoT and AR/VR for enhanced training and fan engagement.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Xbox Game Pass Adds 12 New Titles in March 2026 Wave 2 Including South of Midnight Today
items.summary: Xbox Game Pass is adding 12 new PC and Xbox titles as part of its March 2026 Wave 2, with South of Midnight launching on Cloud, Console, Handheld, and PC today, March 18. Upcoming additions include Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 on April 2, along with updates for Valorant and Sea of Thieves Season 19.

items.title: Xbox Game Pass Announces Late March and April 2026 Additions
items.summary: Xbox has revealed additional games joining Game Pass for the end of March and into April 2026, expanding options for subscribers with new releases and updates.

items.title: Sony Releases Surprise PS3 System Update 4.93
items.summary: Sony deployed PS3 system software update 4.93 on March 18, 2026, marking the first update in exactly one year for the legacy console.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: China's Alibaba and Baidu Raise AI Service Prices Amid Surging Demand
items.summary: Alibaba plans to increase prices for its T-head AI computing chips by 5-34% and storage by 30%, while Baidu will hike AI cloud product prices by up to 30%, effective April 18, due to global demand and supply chain costs.

items.title: Major Tech Layoffs Hit Meta, Amazon, Atlassian, and Others in 2026
items.summary: Companies including Amazon (16,000 jobs), Atlassian (1,600 jobs), Meta (up to 15,000), and Oracle (20,000-30,000) are cutting jobs to pivot toward efficiency and AI infrastructure investments.

items.title: LG Energy Solution Bolsters Leadership in Robotics Battery Market
items.summary: LG Energy Solution is supplying high-energy-density cylindrical batteries and immersion cooling tech to over six major robotics firms, positioning itself for growth as the humanoid robot market surges toward 1.53 million units by 2030.

items.title: One-Third of Organizations Lack Data Foundations Despite HR Tech Investments
items.summary: Rising investments in HR technology have not resolved data gaps, leaving one-third of organizations without reliable workforce analytics for decision-making.
